title: 106 Days of Creation Studies
description: 106 Days of Creation Studies is now out of print, though there may be some copies left on our Scratch & Dent page . Many of the resources used in the lesson plans have become difficult to find. We’re working on a new living science study to replace it, which we’ll announce on our blog and in our weekly e-mails when it’s ready. This award-winning science handbook outlines all the details of the living books, nature study, science experiments, Bible verses, and additional assignments that one family used to study creation with all their elementary children together. (Grades 1–6)

title: 5 Gifts of Intentional Parents video workshop
description: What kind of gifts are you giving your children: survival gifts, default gifts, or intentional gifts? Explore how to be intentional with five gifts that can set your children up for success in adult life.

title: A Beautiful Community video course
description: Building on the free Starting a CMT Group videos, this online 12-video course brings you stories, ideas, and advice from experienced group leaders as it walks you step-by-step through starting your own Charlotte Mason group. Includes the complete CMT Forms & Templates Pack (PDF download)!

title: A Castle with Many Rooms: The Story of the Middle Ages
description: A fabulous living history spine for the whole family. Narrates the story of the Middle Ages around the world from the Fall of Rome to the Renaissance, Reformation, and Scientific Revolution. (Grades 1–12)

title: A Charlotte Mason Companion
description: You can realize the home school of your dreams! A Charlotte Mason Companion is one of the most trusted and referred to books in the home school world. Here are the tools to create an extraordinary learning experience. Karen Andreola’s classic work is brimming with practical how to’s and the inspiration to see them through.

title: A Charlotte Mason Education
description: Key points of Charlotte Mason’s methods presented in a simple and straightforward way. A classic and concise homeschooling how-to manual.

title: A Child’s Copybook Reader
description: This reader and copybook in one gives your student practice in reading and review by writing. Quality selections from classic children’s poetry, Aesop’s fables, the Bible, and more! A great way to help your beginning reader gain fluency and confidence! (Grades 1–3) Coming mid-2025: A new Charlotte Mason handwriting series! Starting with learning letter strokes in Kindergarten and going all the way through cursive practice, this new series will guide you and your student with short, simple-to-follow lessons. title: A Philosophy of Education: Charlotte Mason’s Original Home Schooling Series, Volume 6
description: Volume 6 of Charlotte Mason’s original writings, A Philosophy of Education , explains the solid principles and practical methods of her approach that make education simple yet effective. Purchase the printed copy and receive two e-book formats free!

title: A Pocketful of Pinecones
description: Pocketful of Pinecones is a kind of teacher’s guide in the form of a soothing and uplifting story. Interested in Charlotte Mason’s ideas, Carol (a 1930s homemaker) takes nature walks with her children. She guides them in creating Nature Notebooks. Throughout the four seasons she and her children observe more than 200 living things together. You will find Carol to be busy but not rushed. She loves her husband, home teaches, cooks healthy meals, and understands “make-do and mend” frugality.

title: A Thinking Love (free e-book)

title: A Young Patriot: The American Revolution as Experienced by One Boy
description: A Young Patriot is about a 15-year-old Connecticut farm boy who enlists in the Continental Army in 1776.

description: Discover a way to homeschool that makes learning a delight! Take a look at a typical day in the life of families who use the Charlotte Mason Method in this FREE video workshop. You’ll see what the method looks like in everyday practice and learn how you can make it happen for your own family.

title: It’s a Natural Progression: Language Arts the Charlotte Mason Way video workshop
description: Teaching language arts doesn’t have to be complicated. Discover what exactly is included in language arts and how the Charlotte Mason Method simply follows the natural progression of learning to use language well.

title: Ivanhoe
description: The classic novel by Sir Walter Scott. This unabridged edition pairs well with Great Book Discussions: A Reading Guide for Sir Walter Scott’s Ivanhoe . Recommended for high school literature, grades 9–12.

title: Jack’s Insects
description: Learn fascinating facts about insects as you follow Jack and Maggie’s adventures inside Jack’s new insect book! A classic living science book used in Charlotte Mason’s schools for grades 4-6. (Grades 4–6)

title: Jack’s Insects Narration & Nature Study Notebook
description: New Insects Study Coming Soon Jack’s Insects Narration & Nature Study Notebook is out of print. Coming later in 2026: A Miniature World: Insects and Spiders for Grade 5! This new living science course will use Jack’s Insects alongside other fascinating living books and nature observations. The e-book edition of Jack’s Insects Narration & Nature Study Notebook will be available until A Miniature World: Insects and Spiders is released. Now your student can expand on what he learns from Jack’s Insects. Additional learning opportunities with leading narration questions, prescreened websites and recommended reading, nature study tips, and lots of room to record findings all organized in one attractive notebook. (Grades 4–6) Click the Book List tab to see a list of materials needed for these lesson plans.

title: Jane Eyre
description: The classic novel by Charlotte Brontë. This unabridged edition pairs well with Great Book Discussions: A Reading Guide for Charlotte Brontë’s Jane Eyre . Recommended for high school literature, grades 9–12.

title: Jashub’s Journal: An Old Testament Law Story and Bible Study
description: Legacy Curriculum Simply Charlotte Mason has launched new curriculum for history, geography, and Bible. The lesson plans on this page and other legacy curriculum have been discontinued. Your student will join Jashub and his fellow Israelites as they learn to resolve everyday situations according to God’s Law. This living story combined with a Bible study is designed to highlight God’s wisdom in the laws He made. (Grades 7–12)

If you do not have Oxford First Ancient History , you will want to use the lesson plans in the newer second edition of Joshua through Malachi & Ancient Greece . title: Journaling a Year in Nature
description: A beautiful and sturdy nature journal with gentle prompts to give you ideas of what to look for in nature study throughout each of the four seasons. Inspiring quotes, helpful nature tips, and heavy paper with plenty of room for drawing, painting, and writing about your nature finds. A lovely weekly guide to hours in the out-of-doors! (Grades 1–12)

title: July Video Recording Event in McDonough, GA
description: A Special Live Conference with Sonya Shafer July 15, 2017 McDonough, Georgia This free event will be video recorded. Registration closed.

title: Keep Going with Latin
description: Our recommended Latin course for grades 9 and 10. When purchased through Simply Charlotte Mason, you receive downloadable schedules for Grade 9 and Grade 10 to use Keep Going with Latin in your home school.

title: Keep It Simple
description: Relax. The planning has been done for you! Our lesson plans provide detailed lists of the books you'll need and day-by-day schedules to give your family a whole year of Charlotte Mason learning. Choose your lesson plans in three steps: (1) select your family History Study, (2) add family Enrichment Studies, and (3) select Individual Studies for the grades in which you have students. See below for samples of lesson plans and book lists of needed resources.

title: Keep It Simple: Homeschooling Your Whole Family Together (Free Video Workshop)
description: A free 50-minute workshop video that will show you how to teach your whole family together using Charlotte Mason methods.

title: Keeping Your Balance Calendar Journal
description: Our new personal calendar journal encourages you with Charlotte's ideas to help you keep your balance in the schoolroom, in the living room, and in your heart. Closeout pricing on 2018 Calendar Year edition while supplies last.

title: King Arthur and the Knights of the Round Table Audiobook

title: Know and Tell: The Art of Narration
description: A fantastic parent resource that lays out the natural progression of narration through the grades and confirms its place as a powerful educational and relationship-building method.

title: Lafayette and the American Revolution
description: How the principles of the fledgling United States impacted France. A memorable portrait of one man’s sacrifices for the cause of freedom in two countries.

title: Laying Down the Rails
description: Our bestselling book on habit training! Here, compiled into one volume, are all the habits Charlotte Mason mentioned in her writings with her thoughts and suggestions for cultivating each one. This work also includes Charlotte’s help for breaking bad habits, hundreds of inspiring quotes, and lots of practical tips.

title: Laying Down the Rails + Laying Down the Rails for Children + Laying Down the Rails for Yourself + Workshop Bundle
description: This bundle includes Laying Down the Rails: A Habit-Training Handbook , Laying Down the Rails for Children , Laying Down the Rails for Yourself , and the Laying Down the Rails: The Power of Good Habits workshop at a special discount.

title: Laying Down the Rails + Laying Down the Rails for Children + Workshop Bundle
description: This bundle includes Laying Down the Rails: A Habit-Training Handbook , Laying Down the Rails for Children , and the Laying Down the Rails: The Power of Good Habits workshop at a special discount.

title: Laying Down the Rails for Children: A Habit Training Companion

Corresponds to the award-winning book, Laying Down the Rails. New single-volume edition contains the same material in one book for easier handling!

title: Laying Down the Rails for Yourself
description: The habits book just for adults and young adults. It’s never too late to cultivate good habits! Learn how to lay down some rails of good habits for yourself!

title: Laying Down the Rails Resources (Charlotte Mason Good Habits)
description: Laying Down the Rails is your guide to the formation of good habits that set your child up for lifelong success.

title: Laying Down the Rails: The Power of Good Habits Workshop

Couple this workshop with the Laying Down the Rails complete reference book to get the whole picture.

description: Spark a lifelong appreciation for nature and good books through two Thornton Burgess classics— The Burgess Bird Book for Children and Blacky the Crow —and this companion guide that gives you a leisurely reading schedule, nature study ideas, cross references, and more! (Grades 2–4) Click the Book List tab to see a list of materials needed for these lesson plans.

title: Learning and Living Notebook
description: Now you can share the Learning and Living video series with a group of friends! Extra notebooks are available to make sure that each person who participates in your event or homeschool library can have her own copy (one digital notebook is included with the video series). The 190-page notebook contains helpful lists and information that are referred to in the seminar, plus lots of room to write personal notes. Purchase 10 or more notebooks in the same order and get a 10% bulk order discount!

title: Learning and Living: Homeschooling the Charlotte Mason Way Video Series
description: Get up to speed on the Charlotte Mason Method with this 12-session seminar that will equip, encourage, and inspire you to teach your children with confidence! If you want to fully grasp just how practical and doable the Charlotte Mason style of homeschooling is, Learning and Living: Homeschooling the Charlotte Mason Way is for you!

title: Lessons at Blackberry Inn
description: “Does Carol have her baby?” Readers of Pocketful of Pinecones want to know. This sequel follows Carol as she adjusts to living at Blackberry Inn. She tries some new subjects with her children: Picture Study and Music Appreciation. Drop by the Inn on days of canning, baking, knitting, sewing, and hospitality to revive your enthusiasm for homemaking. All the while you will read how Carol teaches her children with “the gentle art of learning.”

Print books are available while supplies last, and e-books will be available through December 31, 2026. Find out more When Mary went to Egypt to start a school for girls, she never thought she would stay so long. Her letters back to her family in England reveal Egyptian culture, weather, traditions, animals, plants, and more. A fascinating living geography book from 1879 that will touch your mind, your imagination, and your heart. (Grades 4–12)

title: Story Starters
description: This book is out of print and no longer available. Expand your child’s literary powers with the practice of creative narration. Here is your child’s opportunity to rescue those in danger, comfort the sick, cheer the lonely, laugh with the ridiculous, tame the wild, and do battle for good. A story starter suspends a student in the middle of a predicament. He is then faced with the question, “What happens next?” This is his cue to expand and embellish the story however he wishes. He learns to write freely, with imagination and zest. The action-packed stories are especially appealing to boys. To spark even more curiosity, each story is illustrated with engaging 19th century pictures.
